hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'pangis' or 'pangit' — 'pange' is the imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'pangas' — 'pange' is the imperative singular
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'pangere' — 'pange' is the imperative singular

hints.cano, canere, cecini, cantum: These are the principal parts of 'canere' (to sing — a different verb)
hints.pando, pandere, pandi, passum: These are the principal parts of 'pandere' (to spread)
hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are the principal parts of 'laudare' (to praise)

hints.Nominative: While 'lingua' looks nominative, here it is addressed directly — 'Pange, lingua' = 'Sing, O tongue'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'lingua' would also be 'lingua', but here it is the addressee of the imperative
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'lingua' would be 'linguam' — 'lingua' here is vocative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'linguae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'linguae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'linguae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'corpus' — 'corporis' has the -is genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'corpus' would be 'corpori' — 'corporis' ends in -is (genitive)
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'corpus' would be 'corpore' — 'corporis' ends in -is (genitive)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'corporis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'corporis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: While 'mysterium' could be nominative neuter, here it is the direct object of 'pange'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'mysterium' would be 'mysterii' — 'mysterium' is nom./acc. form
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'mysterium' would be 'mysterio' — 'mysterium' is the accusative object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'mysterii' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'mysterii' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'mysterii' ends in -i

translation: Sing, O tongue, the mystery of the glorious body
